text.title: Puppeteer Output Capture (Screenshots, PDFs, HAR) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the puppeteer-output-capture methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Bulk screenshot pipelines MUST use type='jpeg' or 'webp' with quality=80. PNG is allowed only for visual-regression workflows requiring lossless pixels.

rationale.source: Puppeteer screenshot docs + ops cost audit

rule.rationale: Storage and transfer costs dominate high-volume pipelines; lossy formats at quality 80 are visually indistinguishable for most content and 3-5x smaller.

rule.statement: page.pdf() MUST set printBackground=true whenever the page uses background colours or images. Default false strips them.
rule.rationale: Chrome's print CSS hides backgrounds for paper economy; programmatic PDFs of invoices/dashboards need the full design.

rule.statement: page.pdf() MUST be called only after waitForSelector on an anchor element + (when fonts matter) document.fonts.ready. Calling pdf() immediately after goto is forbidden.
rule.rationale: JS-rendered content (charts, fonts, lazy images) may not be finished when goto resolves; the PDF captures a half-rendered page.

rule.statement: HAR output MUST pass through a secret scrubber (Authorization headers, Set-Cookie, JWT bodies, PII patterns) before any persistence step.
rule.rationale: Raw HAR captures Authorization headers and cookies verbatim; persisting them leaks long-lived credentials.

rule.statement: Artifact paths MUST be bounded under a known directory (/tmp/agent-run/<job-id>/) and cleaned up after the job. Writing to arbitrary user-controlled paths is forbidden.
rule.rationale: User-controlled paths enable directory traversal; unbounded paths exhaust disk on long-running daemons.

rule.statement: Captured headers, cookies, and request bodies MUST NOT be written to stdout/stderr without scrubbing. Logging full network state is forbidden.
rule.rationale: stdout often flows into CI logs that are kept long-term and accessible to downstream tools.
